title: Tangy Flaky Norwegian Cheese Flatbread
description: A sour and flaky Norwegian dish featuring shredded Monterey Jack cheese, perfect for midnight cravings, made with long-fermentation-dough and diabetic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Gradually add warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a cool place for 12 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the fermented dough into a large rectangle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Brush the melted butter over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Spread the shredded cheese evenly over the butter, making sure to stay within the border.
Drizzle the Greek yogurt over the cheese and sprinkle with dried dill weed.
Fold the edges of the dough over the filling to form a crust, pressing gently to seal.
Brush the egg over the crust to glaze.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
